page: Microsoft Azure F2amds_v7 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the Famdsv7 server family. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD processors and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, which positions it within the memory-intensive hardware class.

This server type includes 236 GB of local storage and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage for external disk attachments. It does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. There are no integrated graphics processing units on this instance.

From a resource density perspective, the F2amds_v7 offers a high memory allocation relative to its low vCPU count, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads that require significant memory capacity without demanding high parallel processing power. However, the limitation of 2 dedicated vCPUs represents a tradeoff for compute-bound tasks. This virtual machine is configured for memory-intensive workloads, small database hosting, caching layers, and lightweight enterprise applications requiring local storage.
description: Microsoft Azure F2amds_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Famdsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD processors and is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB memory-per-core ratio. Storage capabilities include 236 GB of local disk space alongside compatibility with Premium Storage. This instance does not include a GPU or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive applications that do not require extensive parallel processing. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance, while the local storage capacity supports fast read and write operations for temporary data. The F2amds_v7 is configured for workloads such as small databases, caching systems, and memory-sensitive enterprise applications that benefit from dedicated compute resources and local storage.
